Flavor LibraryRECIPES FOR GREAT PRODUCTS

PIXIAN DOUBANJIANG · SIGNATURE RECIPE

Mapo Tofu

TIME25 minLEVELEasySERVES2–3

Yipin firm tofu cooked in a rich, savory, numbing, and spicy sauce. Pixian doubanjiang gives this classic Sichuan dish its deep color and flavor.

PRODUCTS USED

Four Key Ingredients

INGREDIENTS

What You Need

Yipin firm tofu400 g
Minced beef or pork150–200 g
Pixian doubanjiang1½–2 tbsp
Minced garlic5 cloves
Minced ginger1 tsp
Ground Sichuan pepper½ tsp
Light soy sauce2 tsp, adjust as needed
Corn starch1 tbsp
Cold water for slurry2 tbsp
WaterEnough to just cover the tofu
Scallions and cooking oilAs needed
MSG (optional)½ tsp

METHOD

Start Cooking

  1. 01

    Prepare the tofu

    Cut the Yipin firm tofu into 2 cm cubes. Blanch it in lightly salted boiling water for 1 minute. Lift it out gently and drain.

  2. 02

    Cook the base

    Heat oil in a wok. Add 150–200 g minced beef or pork and cook over low heat until separated and lightly crisp. Add the ginger, garlic, and Pixian doubanjiang. Cook until fragrant and the oil turns red.

  3. 03

    Add water and reduce the sauce

    Add the light soy sauce, then pour in enough water to just cover the tofu. Bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered. Gently push the tofu through the sauce from time to time. Continue cooking until the liquid reduces and the tofu absorbs the flavor.

  4. 04

    Make the slurry and thicken

    In a small bowl, mix 1 tablespoon corn starch with 2 tablespoons cold water until smooth. Pour this starch water into the wok in 2–3 batches. Push the tofu gently after each batch. Wait for the sauce to thicken before adding more.

  5. 05

    Finish and serve

    Stir in ½ teaspoon MSG if using. Turn off the heat when the sauce coats the tofu. Top with Sichuan pepper powder and scallions. Carefully drizzle a little hot oil over the topping, then serve immediately.

Kitchen tips

Pixian doubanjiang is already salty, so taste before adding salt. Use minced beef or pork, depending on what you prefer. Always mix corn starch with cold water before adding it to the hot sauce. Take care when pouring the hot finishing oil.

FEATURED PRODUCT

Pixian
Doubanjiang

Fermented broad bean and chili paste
